daily_brief: The Iran war dominated global attention as President Trump announced US forces could end operations within two to three weeks, while declining responsibility for securing the Strait of Hormuz and telling allies to 'go get your own oil' [Globe and Mail, Financial Times, Le Monde]. The conflict's economic ripple effects are intensifying, with the EU warning of €14 billion in additional energy costs [Financial Times], US gas prices surpassing $4 per gallon, and Asian manufacturing showing signs of strain from supply chain disruptions [Nikkei Asia]. In Lebanon, Israel's defense minister announced plans to establish a permanent buffer zone extending to the Litani River, drawing condemnation from Lebanon and European nations [BBC, Le Monde]. The killing of three Indonesian UN peacekeepers in southern Lebanon within 24 hours underscored the deteriorating security situation [BBC]. Meanwhile, European diplomats gathered in Bucha, Ukraine, to mark four years since the massacre, reaffirming support as the country enters its fifth year of war with Russia [RFI]. On a historic note, India launched its first census in 15 years, beginning the mammoth task of counting over 1.4 billion people [BBC].

top_stories.headline: Trump Signals US Exit from Iran Within Weeks, Refuses to Secure Strait of Hormuz
bullets: President Trump stated US forces would end operations in Iran 'very soon,' estimating two to three weeks, regardless of whether a deal is reached with Tehran
Trump declared securing the Strait of Hormuz is 'not for us' and told allies including France to 'go get your own oil'
The White House announced Trump will deliver a prime-time address Wednesday evening to update the nation on the war
US strikes hit Isfahan, sending a massive fireball into the sky, while Iran attacked a fully loaded Kuwaiti oil tanker in the Persian Gulf

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Emphasized Trump's criticism of allies and market impacts

sources.name: Financial Times
sources.framing_note: Focused on timeline and economic implications

sources.name: Le Monde
sources.framing_note: Highlighted Israeli PM Netanyahu's statement about changing 'the face of the Middle East'

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Emphasized Iran's asymmetric warfare capabilities and US 'maximalist demands'

top_stories.headline: Israel Announces Permanent Buffer Zone in Southern Lebanon, Drawing International Condemnation
bullets: Defense Minister Israel Katz declared Israel will maintain security control over territory up to the Litani River (30km from the border) even after the war ends
Katz announced all houses in Lebanese villages near the Israeli border would be demolished
Lebanon's defense minister called it 'a clear intention to impose a new occupation'; European nations, Canada, and the UN also criticized the announcement
At least 1,238 people have been killed in Lebanon since early March, including 124 children and 52 health workers

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Provided detailed casualty figures and international reaction

sources.name: Le Monde
sources.framing_note: Focused on ongoing strikes and Hezbollah's ground clashes with Israeli forces

top_stories.headline: Three UN Peacekeepers Killed in Lebanon Within 24 Hours as Violence Escalates
bullets: Two Indonesian peacekeepers were killed Monday by a roadside explosion near Bani Hayyan; initial investigation points to an IED
A third Indonesian peacekeeper was killed Sunday when a projectile of unknown origin exploded at a position in Adchit Al Qusayr
A fourth peacekeeper was severely injured in Monday's incident
The deaths came shortly after Israel announced it would step up ground and air attacks against Hezbollah

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Detailed account of UN investigation findings and circumstances

top_stories.headline: European Diplomats Mark Bucha Massacre Anniversary, Reaffirm Ukraine Support
bullets: EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas led a delegation including foreign ministers from Germany, Poland, Italy, Romania, Ireland, Sweden, Bulgaria, and Baltic states to Bucha
The visit marked four years since Russian forces withdrew from Kyiv's suburbs, where hundreds of civilians were found summarily executed
Polish Foreign Minister Sikorski stated anyone claiming Putin is not a war criminal 'should come and see for themselves'
The UN has documented more than 70 summary executions in Bucha

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Emphasized European solidarity and graphic evidence of atrocities

top_stories.headline: India Launches First Census in 15 Years, Counting 1.4 Billion People
bullets: More than three million officials will spend a year counting every person in India across 36 states, over 7,000 sub-districts, and nearly 640,000 villages
The census includes 33 questions covering housing, diet, internet access, and caste data for the first time
The last census was held in 2011; the 2021 round was delayed by the pandemic and administrative issues
India overtook China as the world's most populous country in 2023 according to UN data

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Emphasized scale and policy implications of the exercise

additional_stories.headline: American freelance journalist Shelly Kittleson kidnapped in Baghdad with suspect linked to Iran-backed militia Kataib Hezbollah
EU opens investigation into French state aid for EDF's €73 billion plan to build six new nuclear reactors
Iraq qualifies for FIFA World Cup for first time in 40 years after defeating Bolivia 2-1 in intercontinental playoff
Russian military An-26 plane crashes in Crimea killing all 29 aboard, with technical issues suspected
UNRWA chief Philippe Lazzarini steps down, calls for independent probe into deaths of nearly 400 staff in Gaza
Iran reportedly deploying children as young as 12 in security checkpoint roles amid war
Western Australia invokes emergency powers to force fuel companies to reveal supply chain information amid Middle East crisis
South Korean exports hit all-time monthly high as companies front-load semiconductor orders amid supply chain fears
Pakistan and China announce joint five-point peace initiative for Middle East mediation
NASA's Artemis II mission set to launch first crewed Moon flyby since 1972, featuring first woman and person of color to orbit the Moon
